facts.text: Dance is one of the oldest art forms in human history, used for celebration, storytelling, ritual, and social connection across cultures.
International Dance Day is celebrated every year on April 29, honoring dance as a universal language.
Classical ballet developed from court entertainments in Renaissance Italy and later flourished in France and Russia.
The five basic ballet foot positions are traditionally credited to French choreographer Pierre Beauchamp.
Pointe work lets ballet dancers rise onto the tips of specially reinforced shoes, creating the floating look associated with classical ballet.
The Nutcracker is one of the world's most performed ballets and has become a holiday tradition in many countries.
Modern dance emerged partly as a rebellion against the strict rules of ballet, emphasizing expression and grounded movement.
Martha Graham is considered one of the most influential pioneers of modern dance.
Contemporary dance blends elements of ballet, modern dance, jazz, and improvisation.
Jazz dance grew alongside jazz music in the United States and became a major force in musical theater and pop choreography.
Tap dance combines rhythm, percussion, and footwork, drawing on both African and European dance traditions.
Bill 'Bojangles' Robinson helped bring tap dance to mainstream audiences in film and theater.
Breaking, often called breakdancing, emerged from hip-hop culture in the Bronx during the 1970s.
Breaking made its Olympic debut at the Paris 2024 Games, a huge milestone for street dance.
Salsa dance developed from Cuban and Puerto Rican influences and became especially popular through New York's Latin music scene.
Argentine tango originated in the Río de la Plata region around Buenos Aires and Montevideo.
Tango was added to UNESCO's Intangible Cultural Heritage list in 2009.
Flamenco from Andalusia combines singing, guitar, rhythmic clapping, and intensely expressive dance.
Kathak is a classical dance from India known for spins, storytelling, and intricate footwork.
Bharatanatyam is one of the oldest classical dance traditions of India and is known for precise hand gestures and facial expression.
Hula is a Hawaiian dance tradition that uses movement and chant to tell stories.
Samba is closely associated with Brazil and is one of the signature dance forms of Rio Carnival.
The waltz was once considered scandalous because partners danced in a close embrace instead of standing apart.
The foxtrot is traditionally named after vaudeville performer Harry Fox.
Cha-cha developed in Cuba in the 1950s and is known for its playful triple-step rhythm.
Rumba has deep Afro-Cuban roots and is one of the foundational Latin dance styles.
Merengue is one of the national dances of the Dominican Republic and is often learned as an accessible partner dance.
Bachata also began in the Dominican Republic and grew from a once-marginalized genre into a worldwide dance craze.
Lindy Hop grew out of Harlem's swing era and remains one of the most joyful social partner dances.
Vogue emerged from Harlem ballroom culture and became globally known through both queer performance spaces and pop music.
Belly dance includes several Middle Eastern dance traditions known for torso articulation and expressive isolations.
Line dancing is popular in country and pop settings because it lets many people dance together without needing a partner.
Irish dance gained a huge international boost in the 1990s thanks to the stage phenomenon Riverdance.
Bollywood dance blends Indian classical, folk, and modern pop influences into high-energy group choreography.
K-pop choreography helped make ultra-synchronized group dancing a global pop-culture standard.
Dancers use a technique called spotting during turns to reduce dizziness and keep balance.
Warm-ups are essential in dance because flexible, prepared muscles are less likely to get strained or injured.
Dance training can improve balance, coordination, rhythm, and body awareness.
In partner dancing, 'frame' refers to the posture and connection that help both dancers move together smoothly.
An 8-count phrase is one of the most common ways choreographers structure movement in pop and studio dance.
Dance can be social, ceremonial, theatrical, competitive, or purely for exercise — and many styles overlap all of those purposes.
Zumba was created by Colombian dancer and choreographer Beto Pérez and turned dance fitness into a global phenomenon.
The disco boom of the 1970s made nightclub dancing one of the defining social activities of the decade.
Figure skating and gymnastics both borrow heavily from dance for choreography, musical phrasing, and presentation.
The wedding 'first dance' remains one of the most common dance traditions in modern celebrations.
Labanotation is a formal system for writing down human movement, including dance choreography.
Competitive ballroom dancing is often called dancesport.
Dance/movement therapy uses guided movement to support emotional, physical, and mental well-being.
Dance marathons became a major fad in the 1920s and 1930s, though many were exhausting endurance contests rather than glamorous parties.
Clogging and stepdance focus on percussive footwork, turning the dancer's shoes into a rhythmic instrument.
Capoeira from Brazil blends martial arts, music, and movement so fluidly that it often looks like a dance.
Many traditional dances around the world mark weddings, harvests, religious festivals, and rites of passage.
Social media platforms like TikTok turned short choreographed routines into one of the biggest forms of shared online culture.
Broadway choreography helped make jazz, tap, and theatrical storytelling dance familiar to millions of theatergoers.
Partner dances like salsa, tango, swing, and bachata are often loved as much for connection and musicality as for technical skill.
A lot of dancers say the best part of dancing is that it combines exercise, music, memory, creativity, and confidence all at once.
Popping and locking became major street-dance styles in the 1970s and helped shape later hip-hop choreography.
Krumping is a powerful freestyle street dance style built around explosive expression and high energy.
House dance grew out of club culture and emphasizes fast footwork, grooves, and improvisation.
A pirouette is a spinning turn on one foot and is one of the best-known movements in ballet technique.
A plié — bending the knees while keeping turnout — is one of the fundamental movements in ballet class.
An arabesque is a classic dance position where one leg extends behind the body to create a long, elegant line.
A pas de deux is a dance duet, especially associated with classical ballet.
Bob Fosse became one of the most influential choreographers in American entertainment with his instantly recognizable jazz style.
Merce Cunningham reshaped modern dance by experimenting with chance procedures and unconventional stage relationships.
Pina Bausch helped define dance theatre by blending movement, drama, emotion, and visual design.
The Ballets Russes had a huge influence on 20th-century dance, design, and music far beyond ballet itself.
Swan Lake remains one of the most famous ballets in the world, with music by Tchaikovsky.
The moonwalk became one of pop culture's most famous dance moves after Michael Jackson made it world-famous in 1983.
Bhangra dance, rooted in Punjabi tradition, has become a global party and performance style.
The waltz is danced in triple meter, which gives it its signature sweeping one-two-three feel.
Improvisation is central to many dance forms because it helps dancers respond directly to music, space, and each other.
TV shows like Strictly Come Dancing and Dancing with the Stars helped introduce ballroom terms and styles to huge mainstream audiences.
Ballet became one of the world's most influential formal dance traditions.
Its discipline, pointe work, and storytelling shaped stage dance globally.
Ballet is one of the standout topics people mention when discussing Dancing.
Any overview of Dancing usually includes Ballet.
Ballet helped define the legacy and reputation of Dancing.
Fans exploring Dancing are often encouraged to learn about Ballet.
The story of Dancing is hard to explain without mentioning Ballet.

For many fans, Ballet captures part of why Dancing stays so memorable.
